metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Brawler
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for extended fights vs bruisers, online around minute 24 — about 906 damage over a 20s fight in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +14% more extended fights vs bruisers: try Viper over Elafrost (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Burst
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for one-combo burst, online around minute 24 — about 583 damage in one combo in the sim.
metaBuilds.optimizer: Optimizer sees +62% more one-combo burst: try Viper over Fire Blossom (sim-only — the popular pick may carry utility the sim can't see; test it).

metaBuilds.title: Physical-Def Frontline
metaBuilds.whyLine: Strongest meta core for physical survivability, online around minute 24 — about 9,840 effective HP vs physical in the sim.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Steel deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 29% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Grux deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Bayle deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 36% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Greystone deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 37% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Aurora deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 34% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: They out-threaten you whenever the lane is not even: treat this as a farm-and-team lane, not a duel. Zarus's main threat is Coliseum; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Plasma Blade: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 35% of your damage.

laneSteer.provenance: “Iron Resolve” ⇒ tank; the sim can’t model this augment — steered by the declared playstyle + field evidence — field offlane 47.9% over 898 games, magnitude not simulated.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Khaimera's main threat is Cull; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Serath's main threat is Chastise; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Boris deals area damage — spread out so one cast can't catch the group.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Raiment of Renewal instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 25% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Yin's main threat is Backlash; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: Survive the early checkpoints, concede even trades, and take over once your spike lands. Kwang ramps on auto-attacks — pressure them before they itemize, and mixed resist blunts them later.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Void Helm instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 30% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

matchups.gameplan: You hold the kill-threat edge wherever one exists: play front-foot, but respect jungle attention. Kallari's main threat is Guillotine; bait it before you commit, and armor softens the trade.

counterSwap.line: Losing lane? Third item Warden's Faith instead of Imperator: their 3s all-in loses 33% of its bite; you give up 38% of your damage.

flags: crests and consumables not yet in the build model; augments with tractable mechanics are simulated, the rest are listed unmodeled
